content.text:
## Task 1: Orientation map

**What it is.** Redmine — a Ruby on Rails project management / issue-tracking app. ~1,759 files, mostly Ruby (1,113 files), with ERB views (523) and some JS (122). Standard Rails MVC layout plus a large in-house framework library under `lib/redmine/`.

**How it's organized**

- **Domain models** — `app/models/` (1,945 symbols). The hubs I'll be living in for a visibility/permissions change:
  - `Issue` — `app/models/issue.rb:1` (154 callers)
  - `Project` — `app/models/project.rb:1` (208 callers)
  - `User < Principal` — `app/models/user.rb:1` (324 callers); `AnonymousUser < User`
  - `Member` — `app/models/member.rb:1` (the user↔project↔role join, 218 refs)
  - `Setting` — `app/models/setting.rb:20` (global + plugin config)
  - Plus `Journal`, `Attachment` as supporting actors.

- **Controllers** — `app/controllers/` (681 symbols), all under `ApplicationController` at `app/controllers/application_controller.rb:25`, which is where request-level auth/authorization filters will sit.

- **Helpers / views** — `app/helpers/` (`*Helper` modules, e.g. `ApplicationHelper`) and `app/views/*.erb`.

- **Shared library / framework code** — `lib/redmine/` (1,644 symbols), the engine room. Relevant pieces already visible: `lib/redmine/access_control.rb` (permission registry — central to my change), `lib/redmine/activity.rb`, plus `MenuManager`, `Themes`, `I18n`.

- **Config & routing** — `config/routes.rb` (the entry point), `config/`.
- **Migrations** — `db/migrate/` (971 symbols).
- **Tests** — heaviest area: `test/unit/` (3,111), `test/functional/` (2,249), `test/integration/` (409), all `*_test.rb` using `ActiveSupport::TestCase` / `Redmine::ControllerTest`. The index shows a clean `unit → models` unidirectional dependency, so tests are a reliable oracle for behavior.

**Extension / plugin surface**

- `Redmine::Plugin` — `lib/redmine/plugin.rb:51` is the plugin API (registration, settings, menus, `Migrator#migrate_plugin` at `:525`).
- **Hooks** — `Redmine::Hook` (`lib/redmine/hook.rb`), with `Redmine::Hook::ViewListener` wired into `ApplicationHelper` — how plugins inject view/controller behavior.
- **Permission registration** — `Redmine::AccessControl` (`lib/redmine/access_control.rb`) is where both core and plugins declare permissions; this is the seam any visibility/permission change must respect.
- **Example/reference plugin** — `extra/sample_plugin/` (e.g. `extra/sample_plugin/app/controllers/example_controller.rb:7`) shows the expected plugin layout.

**Carry-forward for the rest of the session:** the permissions/visibility change centers on four collaborating layers — `Redmine::AccessControl` (permission registry) → `Member`/`Role` (grants) → `User` (`allowed_to?`-style checks) → `Issue`/`Project` (`visible` scopes), enforced at the controller layer via `ApplicationController` filters, with `Redmine::Plugin` + `Redmine::Hook` as the third-party surface that must keep working. I'll drill into each next.

Ready for Task 2.

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2

18	# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
19	
20	class Issue < ApplicationRecord
21	  include Redmine::SafeAttributes
22	  include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation
23	  include Redmine::I18n
24	
25	  before_validation :default_assign, on: :create
26	  before_validation :force_default_value_on_noneditable_custom_fields, on: :create
27	  before_validation :clear_disabled_fields
28	  before_save :set_parent_id
29	  include Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet
30	  include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable
31	
32	  belongs_to :project
33	  belongs_to :tracker
34	  belongs_to :status, :class_name => 'IssueStatus'
35	  belongs_to :author, :class_name => 'User'
36	  belongs_to :assigned_to, :class_name => 'Principal'
37	  belongs_to :fixed_version, :class_name => 'Version'
38	  belongs_to :priority, :class_name => 'IssuePriority'
39	  belongs_to :category, :class_name => 'IssueCategory'
40

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Redmine - project management software
# Copyright (C) 2006-  Jean-Philippe Lang
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.

class Issue < ApplicationRecord
  include Redmine::SafeAttributes
  include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation
  include Redmine::I18n

  before_validation :default_assign, on: :create
  before_validation :force_default_value_on_noneditable_custom_fields, on: :create
  before_validation :clear_disabled_fields
  before_save :set_parent_id
  include Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet
  include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable

  belongs_to :project
  belongs_to :tracker
  belongs_to :status, :class_name => 'IssueStatus'
  belongs_to :author, :class_name => 'User'
  belongs_to :assigned_to, :class_name => 'Principal'
  belongs_to :fixed_version, :class_name => 'Version'
  belongs_to :priority, :class_name => 'IssuePriority'
  belongs_to :category, :class_name => 'IssueCategory'

  has_many :journals, :as => :journalized, :dependent => :destroy, :inverse_of => :journalized
  has_many :time_entries, :dependent => :destroy
  has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets, lambda {order("#{Changeset.table_name}.committed_on ASC, #{Changeset.table_name}.id ASC")}

  has_many :relations_from, :class_name => 'IssueRelation', :foreign_key => 'issue_from_id', :dependent => :delete_all
  has_many :relations_to, :class_name => 'IssueRelation', :foreign_key => 'issue_to_id', :dependent => :delete_all

  acts_as_attachable :after_add => :attachment_added, :after_remove => :attachment_removed
  acts_as_customizable
  acts_as_watchable
  acts_as_searchable :columns => ['subject', "#{table_name}.description"],
                     :preload => [:project, :status, :tracker],
                     :scope => lambda {|options| options[:open_issues] ? self.open : self.all}

  acts_as_event :title => Proc.new {|o| "#{o.tracker.name} ##{o.id} (#{o.status}): #{o.subject}"},
                :url => Proc.new {|o| {:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => o.id}},
                :type => Proc.new {|o| 'issue' + (o.closed? ? '-closed' : '')}

  acts_as_activity_provider :scope => proc {preload(:project, :author, :tracker, :status)},
                            :author_key => :author_id

  acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']
  acts_as_webhookable
  include Issue::Webhookable

  DONE_RATIO_OPTIONS = %w(issue_field issue_status)

  attr_reader :transition_warning
  attr_writer :deleted_attachment_ids
  delegate :notes, :notes=, :private_notes, :private_notes=, :to => :current_journal, :allow_nil => true

  validates_presence_of :subject, :project, :tracker
  validates_presence_of :priority, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.priority_id_changed?}
  validates_presence_of :status, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.status_id_changed?}
  validates_presence_of :author, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.author_id_changed?}

  validates_length_of :subject, :maximum => 255
  validates_inclusion_of :done_ratio, :in => 0..100
  validates :estimated_hours, :numericality => {:greater_than_or_equal_to => 0, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid}
  validates :start_date, :date => true
  validates :due_date, :date => true
  validate :validate_issue, :validate_required_fields, :validate_permissions

  scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
    joins(:project).
    where(Issue.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
  end)

  scope :open, (lambda do |*args|
    is_closed = !args.empty? ? !args.first : false
    joins(:status).
    where(:issue_statuses => {:is_closed => is_closed})
  end)

  scope :recently_updated, lambda {order(:updated_on => :desc)}
  scope :on_active_project, (lambda do
    joins(:project).
    where(:projects => {:status => Project::STATUS_ACTIVE})
  end)
  scope :fixed_version, (lambda do |versions|
    ids = [versions].flatten.compact.map {|v| v.is_a?(Version) ? v.id : v}
    ids.any? ? where(:fixed_version_id => ids) : none
  end)
  scope :assigned_to, (lambda do |arg|
    arg = Array(arg).uniq
    ids = arg.map {|p| p.is_a?(Principal) ? p.id : p}
    ids += arg.grep(User).map(&:group_ids).flatten.uniq
    ids.compact!
    ids.any? ? where(:assigned_to_id => ids) : none
  end)
  scope :like, (lambda do |q|
    if q.present?
      where(*::Query.tokenized_like_conditions("#{table_name}.subject", q))
    end
  end)

  before_save :close_duplicates, :update_done_ratio_from_issue_status,
              :force_updated_on_change, :update_closed_on
  after_save do |issue|
    if !issue.saved_change_to_id? && issue.saved_change_to_project_id?

# Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit or add notes to the issue
  def editable?(user=User.current)
    attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit the issue
  def attributes_editable?(user=User.current)
    user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_issues) || (
      user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_own_issues) && author == user
    )
  end

  def attachments_addable?(user=User.current)
    attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Attachable::InstanceMethods#attachments_editable?
  def attachments_editable?(user=User.current)
    visible?(user) && attributes_editable?(user)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to add notes to the issue
  def notes_addable?(user=User.current)
    user_tracker_permission?(user, :add_issue_notes)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to delete the issue
  def deletable?(user=User.current)
    user_tracker_permission?(user, :delete_issues)
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Attachable::InstanceMethods#attachments_deletable?
  def attachments_deletable?(user=User.current)
    visible?(user) && attributes_editable?(user)
  end

  def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
    super
    if new_record?
      # set default values for new records only
      self.priority ||= IssuePriority.default
      self.watcher_user_ids = []
    end
  end

  def create_or_update(*args)
    super()
  ensure
    @status_was = nil
  end
  private :create_or_update

  # AR#Persistence#destroy would raise and RecordNotFound exception
  # if the issue was already deleted or updated (non matching lock_version).
  # This is a problem when bulk deleting issues or deleting a project
  # (because an issue may already be deleted if its parent was deleted
  # first).
  # The issue is reloaded by the nested_set before being deleted so
  # the lock_version condition should not be an issue but we handle it.
  def destroy
    super
  rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    # Stale or already deleted
    begin
      reload
    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
      # The issue was actually already deleted
      @destroyed = true
      return freeze
    end
    # The issue was stale, retry to destroy
    super
  end

  alias :base_reload :reload
  def reload(*)
    @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
    @assignable_versions = nil
    @relations = nil
    @spent_hours = nil
    @total_spent_hours = nil
    @total_estimated_hours = nil
    @last_updated_by = nil
    @last_notes = nil
    base_reload(*)
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods#available_custom_fields
  def available_custom_fields
    (project && tracker) ? (project.all_issue_custom_fields & tracker.custom_fields) : []
  end

  def visible_custom_field_values(user=nil)
    user_real = user || User.current

## Task 2: The `Issue` contract

`Issue < ApplicationRecord` lives in `app/models/issue.rb:20` and is a 2,150-line behemoth. Here's everything a teammate must know before touching it.

### Associations (`app/models/issue.rb:32–46`)
- `belongs_to`: `project`, `tracker`, `status` (IssueStatus), `author` (User), `assigned_to` (**Principal** — user *or* group), `fixed_version` (Version), `priority`, `category`.
- `has_many`: `journals` (polymorphic `:as => :journalized`, the audit trail), `time_entries`.
- `has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets` — repository linkage.
- Self-referential graph: `relations_from` / `relations_to` (`IssueRelation`), plus the parent/child tree (see nested-set below).

Note for the visibility change: `assigned_to` is a `Principal`, so any "assigned to me" visibility rule must account for **group** membership (the `assigned_to` scope at `:104` already expands `group_ids`).

### Shared `acts_as_*` plugin-style behaviors (`:48–63`)
Each is a Rails plugin under `lib/plugins/`, mixed in at class load:
- `acts_as_attachable` — `lib/plugins/acts_as_attachable/lib/acts_as_attachable.rb:52`. **Carries its own permission gates** (`attachments_visible?`, `attachments_deletable?`) that `Issue` overrides at `:218,:234` to defer to `visible?` — directly relevant to the change.
- `acts_as_customizable` — `lib/plugins/acts_as_customizable/lib/acts_as_customizable.rb:28` (custom fields; `available_custom_fields` overridden at `:289`).
- `acts_as_watchable` — `lib/plugins/acts_as_watchable/lib/acts_as_watchable.rb:29` (watchers; watchers can be a visibility basis).
- `acts_as_searchable` — `lib/plugins/acts_as_searchable/lib/acts_as_searchable.rb:39` (its `:scope` lambda feeds search; **search has to re-apply visibility**).
- `acts_as_event` — `lib/plugins/acts_as_event/lib/acts_as_event.rb:28` (activity-stream rendering).
- `acts_as_activity_provider` — `lib/plugins/acts_as_activity_provider/lib/acts_as_activity_provider.rb:28` (**activity feeds query issues directly and must enforce visibility independently**).
- `acts_as_mentionable` — `lib/redmine/acts/mentionable.rb:28`.
- `acts_as_webhookable` + `include Issue::Webhookable` — `lib/redmine/acts/webhookable.rb` / `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20` (outbound notifications — must not leak invisible issues).

### Cross-cutting mixins / concerns (`:21–30, :62–64`)
- `Redmine::SafeAttributes` — `lib/redmine/safe_attributes.rb:21`. **Critical.** Mass-assignment is permission-gated via `safe_attributes` declarations (`app/models/issue.rb:498–565`) and `safe_attributes=` (`:565`). Visibility of *fields* (e.g. private notes, custom fields) flows through here.
- `Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` — `lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb:22` (the parent/child tree; lock_version/`set_parent_id` machinery, and why `destroy` is overridden at `:260`).
- `Redmine::Reaction::Reactable` — `lib/redmine/reaction.rb:35`.
- `Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation` — `lib/redmine/utils/date_calculation.rb:22` (scheduling).
- `Redmine::I18n` — `lib/redmine/i18n.rb:23`.

### The visibility surface (the heart of the upcoming change)
- **Class scope** `scope :visible` — `app/models/issue.rb:84`, delegates to…
- **`self.visible_condition(user, options={})`** — `app/models/issue.rb:137`: builds the SQL WHERE clause from the user's roles/`issues_visibility` per project. This is the SQL-level enforcement.
- **`visible?(usr=nil)`** — `app/models/issue.rb:172`: the in-memory per-record check (must stay consistent with `visible_condition`).
- Permission helpers driving editability: `attributes_editable?` `:207`, `notes_addable?` `:223`, `deletable?` `:228`, all routed through `user_tracker_permission?` — tracker-scoped permission checks (the layer I'll dig into in the role/permission tasks).

**Carry-forward:** the issue's visibility is enforced in *two places that must agree* (`visible_condition` SQL at `:137` and `visible?` Ruby at `:172`), and **leaks past those two** through at least four independent query paths — `acts_as_searchable`, `acts_as_activity_provider`, attachments, and webhooks. Any change to the visibility model has to be threaded through all of them, plus `SafeAttributes` for field-level visibility. Next I'll trace where `visible_condition` gets its permission/role data.

Ready for Task 3.

- **`self.visible_condition(user, options={})`** — `app/models/issue.rb:137`: builds the SQL WHERE clause from the user's roles/`issues_visibility` per project. This is the SQL-level enforcement.
- **`visible?(usr=nil)`** — `app/models/issue.rb:172`: the in-memory per-record check (must stay consistent with `visible_condition`).
- Permission helpers driving editability: `attributes_editable?` `:207`, `notes_addable?` `:223`, `deletable?` `:228`, all routed through `user_tracker_permission?` — tracker-scoped permission checks (the layer I'll dig into in the role/permission tasks).

**Carry-forward:** the issue's visibility is enforced in *two places that must agree* (`visible_condition` SQL at `:137` and `visible?` Ruby at `:172`), and **leaks past those two** through at least four independent query paths — `acts_as_searchable`, `acts_as_activity_provider`, attachments, and webhooks. Any change to the visibility model has to be threaded through all of them, plus `SafeAttributes` for field-level visibility. Next I'll trace where `visible_condition` gets its permission/role data.

Ready for Task 3.

A change to `visible_condition`/`visible?` propagates correctly only if `IssueQuery#base_scope` and these parallel paths are all updated in lockstep. Test coverage is dense (`IssuesControllerTest` alone spans `issues_controller_test.rb:22–9268`), so regressions should surface — `AttachmentsVisibilityTest`, the issue-relations visibility test, and the activities private-notes test are the canaries.

# Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
  def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
    Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
      sql =
        if user.id && user.logged?
          case role.issues_visibility
          when 'all'
            '1=1'
          when 'default'
            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
            "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
              "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
              "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
          when 'own'
            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
            "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
              "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
          else
            '1=0'
          end
        else
          "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
        end
      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
        tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
        if tracker_ids.any?
          sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
        else
          sql = '1=0'
        end
      end
      sql
    end
  end

  # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
  def visible?(usr=nil)
    (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
      visible =
        if user.logged?
          case role.issues_visibility
          when 'all'
            true
          when 'default'
            !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
          when 'own'
            self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
          else
            false
          end
        else
          !self.is_private?
        end
      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
        visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
      end
      visible
    end
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to log time on the issue
  def time_loggable?(user=User.current)
    user.allowed_to?(:log_time, project) && (Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? || !closed?)
  end

# * :skip_pre_condition => true       don't check that the module is enabled (eg. when the condition is already set elsewhere in the query)
  # * :project => project               limit the condition to project
  # * :with_subprojects => true         limit the condition to project and its subprojects
  # * :member => true                   limit the condition to the user projects
  def self.allowed_to_condition(user, permission, options={})
    perm = Redmine::AccessControl.permission(permission)
    base_statement =
      if perm && perm.read?
        "#{Project.table_name}.status IN (#{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}, #{Project::STATUS_CLOSED})"
      else
        "#{Project.table_name}.status = #{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}"
      end
    if !options[:skip_pre_condition] && perm && perm.project_module
      # If the permission belongs to a project module, make sure the module is enabled
      base_statement +=
        " AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 AS one FROM #{EnabledModule.table_name} em" \
          " WHERE em.project_id = #{Project.table_name}.id" \
          " AND em.name='#{perm.project_module}')"
    end
    if project = options[:project]
      project_statement = project.project_condition(options[:with_subprojects])
      base_statement = "(#{project_statement}) AND (#{base_statement})"
    end

    if user.admin?
      base_statement
    else
      statement_by_role = {}
      unless options[:member]
        role = user.builtin_role
        if role.allowed_to?(permission)
          s = "#{Project.table_name}.is_public = #{connection.quoted_true}"
          if user.id
            group = role.anonymous? ? Group.anonymous : Group.non_member
            principal_ids = [user.id, group.id].compact
            s =
              "(#{s} AND #{Project.table_name}.id NOT IN " \
                "(SELECT project_id FROM #{Member.table_name} " \
                "WHERE user_id IN (#{principal_ids.join(',')})))"
          end
          statement_by_role[role] = s
        end
      end
      user.project_ids_by_role.each do |role, project_ids|
        if role.allowed_to?(permission) && project_ids.any?
          statement_by_role[role] = "#{Project.table_name}.id IN (#{project_ids.join(',')})"
        end
      end
      if statement_by_role.empty?
        "1=0"
      else
        if block_given?
          statement_by_role.each do |role, statement|
            if s = yield(role, user)
              statement_by_role[role] = "(#{statement} AND (#{s}))"
            end
          end
        end
        "((#{base_statement}) AND (#{statement_by_role.values.join(' OR ')}))"
      end
    end
  end

  def override_roles(role)
    @override_members ||= memberships.
      joins(:principal).
      where(:users => {:type => ['GroupAnonymous', 'GroupNonMember']}).to_a

    group_class = role.anonymous? ? GroupAnonymous : GroupNonMember
    member = @override_members.detect {|m| m.principal.is_a? group_class}
    member ? member.roles.to_a : [role]
  end

  def principals
    @principals ||=

content.text:
## Task 4: How the code decides "may user X see issue Y"

There are **three layers**, and they all pivot on one shared primitive: `Project.allowed_to_condition`.

### Layer 0 — the shared engine: `Project.allowed_to_condition`
`app/models/project.rb:189`. This is the foundation everything else builds on. Given `(user, permission, options)` it produces a SQL fragment selecting projects where the user holds `permission`, then **yields `(role, user)` to a block so the caller can AND in record-level rules per role** (`:236–242`). Key mechanics:
- Project must be active/closed and (if the permission belongs to a module) the **module must be enabled** — `:197–203`.
- `user.admin?` → unconditional pass (`:209–210`).
- Otherwise it assembles `statement_by_role` from the user's **builtin role** (anonymous/non-member, public-project access at `:214–226`) plus `user.project_ids_by_role` (`:228–232`).
- Empty → `"1=0"` (sees nothing); else `(base AND (role1cond OR role2cond ...))` (`:243`).

The per-record analog of this is `User#allowed_to?(permission, project) { |role, user| ... }` — same block contract, evaluated in Ruby instead of SQL.

### Layer 1 — the DB-level condition: `Issue.visible_condition`
`app/models/issue.rb:137`. Calls `Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options)` and supplies the block (`:138–168`) that encodes the **issue visibility rule keyed off `role.issues_visibility`**:
- `'all'` → `1=1` (every issue in allowed projects) — `:142`.
- `'default'` → not private **OR** author is me **OR** assigned to me/my groups — `:144–148`.
- `'own'` → author or assignee is me/my groups — `:149–152`.
- else → `1=0` — `:154`.
- Anonymous/not-logged-in → only non-private issues — `:156–157`.
- Then, **tracker-scoped narrowing**: if the role doesn't have `view_issues` on all trackers, AND `tracker_id IN (...)` the permitted trackers, else `1=0` — `:159–166`.

This SQL is what `scope :visible` (`issue.rb:84`) injects, and it's what `IssueQuery#base_scope` (`issue_query.rb:375`, `Issue.visible`) relies on for every list.

### Layer 2 — the per-record check: `Issue#visible?`
`app/models/issue.rb:172`. The in-memory mirror of Layer 1: `(usr||User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, project) { |role, user| ... }` with a block (`:174–192`) that **must stay logically identical to `visible_condition`**:
- same `issues_visibility` cases — `'all'`/`'default'`/`'own'` (`:177–185`), using `is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)` (the group-aware form of the SQL's `assigned_to_id IN (...)`).
- anonymous → `!is_private?` (`:187`).
- same tracker narrowing via `role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)` (`:189–190`).

**This duplication is the central hazard of the planned change**: the SQL builder (`:137`) and the Ruby check (`:172`) hand-encode the *same* rule twice and can silently diverge.

### Where the same visibility idea recurs (same `visible_condition` + `visible?` pair shape)
- **`Project`** — `visible?` `app/models/project.rb:168`, `self.visible_condition` `:178` (the base case: public projects + memberships).
- **`TimeEntry`** — `visible_condition` `app/models/time_entry.rb:87`, `visible?` `:100` (uses `:view_time_entries` and, like issues, has an `own`/`all` time-entry-visibility role split; also re-checks issue privacy).
- **Custom fields** — the parallel "which *fields* can this user see" condition: `CustomField#visibility_by_project_condition` `app/models/custom_field.rb:278`, specialized in `IssueCustomField#visibility_by_project_condition` `app/models/issue_custom_field.rb:35` and `ProjectCustomField` `:29`. Role-driven, same project/role machinery.
- **Record-level `visible?` (Ruby-only, delegating to `User#allowed_to?`)** on the other content types — `News` `app/models/news.rb:53`, `Document` `app/models/document.rb:55`, `Message` `app/models/message.rb:78`, `WikiPage` `app/models/wiki_page.rb:89`, `Journal#visible?` `app/models/journal.rb:165` (which also gates **private notes** via `visible_details` from Task 3).
- **Query subclasses** carry the SQL versions of role-scoped visibility: `IssueQuery#sql_for_assigned_to_role_field` `app/models/issue_query.rb:599`, `sql_for_author_role_field:637`; `TimeEntryQuery#sql_for_user_role_field` `app/models/time_entry_query.rb:309`.

### Carry-forward
The whole system is **one primitive (`Project.allowed_to_condition` / `User#allowed_to?`) + per-model blocks that encode each record type's rule twice (SQL + Ruby)**. For issues specifically, the rule = *role's `issues_visibility` setting* × *privacy/author/assignee* × *per-tracker permission*. The next tasks need to nail down where `role.issues_visibility`, `permissions_all_trackers?`, and `project_ids_by_role` come from — i.e. the Role/Member/AccessControl layer that feeds all of this. The single biggest correctness risk for the change is keeping the SQL builder (`issue.rb:137`) and the Ruby checker (`issue.rb:172`) — and their five sibling models — in agreement.

Ready for Task 5.

  ]

  has_many :projects, foreign_key: 'default_issue_query_id', dependent: :nullify, inverse_of: 'default_issue_query'
  after_update { projects.clear unless visibility == VISIBILITY_PUBLIC }
  scope :for_all_projects, ->{ where(project_id: nil) }

  def self.default(project: nil, user: User.current)
    # user default
    if user&.logged? && (query_id = user.pref.default_issue_query).present?
      query = find_by(id: query_id)
      return query if query&.visible?(user)
    end

    # project default
    query = project&.default_issue_query
    return query if query&.visibility == VISIBILITY_PUBLIC

    # global default
    if (query_id = Setting.default_issue_query).present?
      query = find_by(id: query_id)
      return query if query&.visibility == VISIBILITY_PUBLIC
    end
    nil
  end

  def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
    super(attributes)
    self.filters ||= {'status_id' => {:operator => "o", :values => [""]}}
  end

  def draw_relations
    r = options[:draw_relations]
    r.nil? || r == '1'
  end

  def draw_relations=(arg)
    options[:draw_relations] = (arg == '0' ? '0' : nil)
  end

  def draw_progress_line
    r = options[:draw_progress_line]
    r == '1'
  end

  def draw_progress_line=(arg)
    options[:draw_progress_line] = (arg == '1' ? '1' : nil)
  end

  def draw_selected_columns
    r = options[:draw_selected_columns]
    r == '1'
  end

  def draw_selected_columns=(arg)
    options[:draw_selected_columns] = (arg == '1' ? '1' : nil)
  end

  def build_from_params(params, defaults={})
    super
    self.draw_relations =
      params[:draw_relations] ||
        (params[:query] && params[:query][:draw_relations]) || options[:draw_relations]
    self.draw_progress_line =
      params[:draw_progress_line] ||
        (params[:query] && params[:query][:draw_progress_line]) ||
        options[:draw_progress_line]
    self.draw_selected_columns =
      params[:draw_selected_columns] ||
        (params[:query] && params[:query][:draw_selected_columns]) ||
        options[:draw_progress_line]
    self
  end

content.text:
## Task 5: What produces the issue-query column set

The column set is produced by **`IssueQuery#available_columns`** — `app/models/issue_query.rb:302`. It is *not* a static list; it composes four sources and then filters. Tracing the body:

### 1. Static base set — `self.class.available_columns.dup` (`issue_query.rb:305`)
The class-level array declared at `app/models/issue_query.rb:25–79`. Each entry is a `QueryColumn` (or subclass), and the cross-model reach is in the column definitions themselves:
- `Issue` table columns — id, subject, dates, done_ratio, etc.
- `Project` (`:28`), `Tracker` (`:29`, `Tracker.table_name`), `IssueStatus` (`:34`), `IssuePriority` (`:35`), `IssueCategory` (`:47`).
- `:author`/`:assigned_to`/`:last_updated_by` → **`User.fields_for_order_statement`** (`:39,:42,:74`).
- `:fixed_version` → **`Version.fields_for_order_statement`** (`:48`).
- `WatcherQueryColumn` for `:watcher_users` (`:44`) → reaches the watchers/`Watcher` join.
- **Non-obvious**: `:total_estimated_hours` (`:57–67`) embeds a subquery that calls **`Issue.visible_condition(User.current)`** (`:62`) — so even building the *static* column list pulls in the entire Task-4 visibility machinery (and string-rewrites `issues`→`subtasks`).

### 2. Custom-field columns — `issue_custom_fields.visible` (`issue_query.rb:306`)
`issue_custom_fields` is defined in the parent `Query` at `app/models/query.rb:692`, and **branches on project**:
- project present → **`project.rolled_up_custom_fields`** (`query.rb:694`) — walks the project + subproject hierarchy's enabled trackers/fields.
- no project → **`IssueCustomField.sorted`** (`query.rb:696`).

Then `.visible` — the **`CustomField` scope at `app/models/custom_field.rb:56`**, whose role/project visibility is built by `IssueCustomField#visibility_by_project_condition` (`app/models/issue_custom_field.rb:35`, overriding `custom_field.rb:278`). Each survivor is wrapped in a `QueryCustomFieldColumn`. → This is the **second visibility system** (field-level, from Task 4) reached transitively from a method whose signature says nothing about it.

### 3. Permission-gated dynamic columns
- **Spent-time columns** (`issue_query.rb:308–339`): only if `User.current.allowed_to?(:view_time_entries, project, :global => true)`. Their `:sortable` SQL embeds a subselect joining `TimeEntry` + `Project` and gated by **`TimeEntry.visible_condition(User.current)`** (`:315`, `:329`) — pulling in TimeEntry's *own* visibility condition (Task 4 sibling). `:total_spent_hours` additionally self-joins `Issue` on the nested-set columns (`root_id/lft/rgt`).

The column builder is a **second consumer of the visibility model** (Task 4), reached three ways that aren't visible in the signature: `Issue.visible_condition` inside the static `total_estimated_hours` SQL, `TimeEntry.visible_condition` inside the conditional spent-hours columns, and `CustomField#visibility_by_project_condition` via `issue_custom_fields.visible`. # frozen_string_literal: true

# Redmine - project management software
# Copyright (C) 2006-  Jean-Philippe Lang
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.

class TimeEntry < ApplicationRecord
  include Redmine::SafeAttributes
  # could have used polymorphic association
  # project association here allows easy loading of time entries at project level with one database trip
  belongs_to :project
  belongs_to :issue
  belongs_to :user
  belongs_to :author, :class_name => 'User'
  belongs_to :activity, :class_name => 'TimeEntryActivity'

  acts_as_customizable
  acts_as_event(
    :title =>
      Proc.new do |o|
        related   = o.issue if o.issue && o.issue.visible?
        related ||= o.project
        "#{l_hours(o.hours)} (#{related.event_title})"
      end,
    :url =>
      Proc.new do |o|
        {:controller => 'timelog', :action => 'index', :project_id => o.project, :issue_id => o.issue}
      end,
    :author => :user,
    :group => :issue,
    :description => :comments
  )
  acts_as_activity_provider :timestamp => "#{table_name}.created_on",
                            :author_key => :user_id,
                            :scope => proc {joins(:project).preload(:project)}
  acts_as_webhookable

  validates_presence_of :author_id, :user_id, :activity_id, :project_id, :hours, :spent_on
  validates_presence_of :issue_id, :if => lambda {Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('issue_id')}
  validates_presence_of :comments, :if => lambda {Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('comments')}
  validates_numericality_of :hours, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid
  validates_length_of :comments, :maximum => 1024, :allow_nil => true
  validates :spent_on, :date => true
  before_validation :set_project_if_nil
  # TODO: remove this, author should be always explicitly set
  before_validation :set_author_if_nil
  validate :validate_time_entry

  scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
    joins(:project).
    where(TimeEntry.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
  end)
  scope :left_join_issue, (lambda do
    joins(
      "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{Issue.table_name}" \
      " ON #{Issue.table_name}.id = #{TimeEntry.table_name}.issue_id" \
      " AND (#{Issue.visible_condition(User.current)})"
    )
  end)
  scope :on_issue, (lambda do |issue|
    joins(:issue).
    where("#{Issue.table_name}.root_id = #{issue.root_id} AND #{Issue.table_name}.lft >= #{issue.lft} AND #{Issue.table_name}.rgt <= #{issue.rgt}")
  end)

  safe_attributes 'user_id', 'hours', 'comments', 'project_id',
                  'issue_id', 'activity_id', 'spent_on',
                  'custom_field_values', 'custom_fields'

  def webhook_payload_api_template
    "app/views/timelog/show.api.rsb"
  end

  # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all time entries visible by the specified user
  def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
    Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_time_entries, options) do |role, user|
      if role.time_entries_visibility == 'all'
        nil
      elsif role.time_entries_visibility == 'own' && user.id && user.logged?
        "#{table_name}.user_id = #{user.id}"
      else
        '1=0'
      end
    end
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to view the time entry
  def visible?(user=nil)
    (user || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_time_entries, self.project) do |role, user|
      if role.time_entries_visibility == 'all'
        true
      elsif role.time_entries_visibility == 'own'
        self.user == user
      else
        false
      end
    end
  end

  def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
    super
    if new_record? && self.activity.nil?
      self.activity_id = TimeEntryActivity.default_activity_id(User.current, self.project)
      self.hours = nil if hours == 0
    end
  end

  def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)

file.content:
def spent_hours
    @spent_hours ||= time_entries.sum(:hours) || 0.0
  end

  # Returns the total number of hours spent on this issue and its descendants
  def total_spent_hours
    @total_spent_hours ||=
      if leaf?
        spent_hours
      else
        self_and_descendants.joins(:time_entries).sum("#{TimeEntry.table_name}.hours").to_f
      end
  end

  def total_estimated_hours
    if leaf?
      estimated_hours
    else
      @total_estimated_hours ||= self_and_descendants.visible.sum(:estimated_hours)
    end
  end

  # Returns the number of estimated remaining hours on this issue
  def estimated_remaining_hours
    (estimated_hours || 0) * (100 - (done_ratio || 0)) / 100
  end

  def relations
    @relations ||= IssueRelation::Relations.new(
      self,
      IssueRelation.where('issue_from_id = ? OR issue_to_id = ?', id, id).sort
    )
  end

  def last_updated_by
    if @last_updated_by
      @last_updated_by.presence
    else
      journals.reorder(:id => :desc).first.try(:user)
    end
  end

  def last_notes
    @last_notes || journals.visible.where.not(notes: '').reorder(:id => :desc).first.try(:notes)
  end

  # Preloads relations for a collection of issues
  def self.load_relations(issues)
    if issues.any?
      relations =
        IssueRelation.where(
          "issue_from_id IN (:ids) OR issue_to_id IN (:ids)", :ids => issues.map(&:id)
        ).all
      issues.each do |issue|
        issue.instance_variable_set(
          :@relations,
          relations.select {|r| r.issue_from_id == issue.id || r.issue_to_id == issue.id}
        )
      end
    end
  end

  # Preloads visible spent time for a collection of issues
  def self.load_visible_spent_hours(issues, user=User.current)
    if issues.any?
      hours_by_issue_id = TimeEntry.visible(user).where(:issue_id => issues.map(&:id)).group(:issue_id).sum(:hours)
      issues.each do |issue|
        issue.instance_variable_set :@spent_hours, (hours_by_issue_id[issue.id] || 0.0)
      end
    end
  end

  # Preloads visible total spent time for a collection of issues
  def self.load_visible_total_spent_hours(issues, user=User.current)
    if issues.any?
      hours_by_issue_id = TimeEntry.visible(user).joins(:issue).
        joins("JOIN #{Issue.table_name} parent ON parent.root_id = #{Issue.table_name}.root_id" +
          " AND parent.lft <= #{Issue.table_name}.lft AND parent.rgt >= #{Issue.table_name}.rgt").
        where("parent.id IN (?)", issues.map(&:id)).group("parent.id").sum(:hours)
      issues.each do |issue|
        issue.instance_variable_set :@total_spent_hours, (hours_by_issue_id[issue.id] || 0.0)
      end
    end
  end

  # Preloads visible relations for a collection of issues
  def self.load_visible_relations(issues, user=User.current)
    if issues.any?
      issue_ids = issues.map(&:id)
      # Relations with issue_from in given issues and visible issue_to
      relations_from = IssueRelation.joins(:issue_to => :project).
                         where(visible_condition(user)).where(:issue_from_id => issue_ids).to_a
      # Relations with issue_to in given issues and visible issue_from
      relations_to = IssueRelation.joins(:issue_from => :project).
                         where(visible_condition(user)).

content.text:
## Task 6: Time logging — model, aggregation, and visibility gates

### How a time record connects to an issue and project
`TimeEntry < ApplicationRecord` — `app/models/time_entry.rb:20`.
- **Not polymorphic** — it carries *both* `belongs_to :issue` and `belongs_to :project` directly (`time_entry.rb:24–25`). The comment at `:22–23` explains this is deliberate: the redundant `project_id` lets project-level rollups load in one DB trip without joining issues. `issue_id` is nullable (time can be logged at project level); `project_id` is always present (`set_project_if_nil` at `:57`, `validates_presence_of :project_id` at `:51`).
- `belongs_to :user` (who the time is *for*) vs `belongs_to :author` (who entered it) — `:26–27`. The visibility "own" rule keys off `user_id`, not `author_id`.
- Scopes that wire it to issues: `on_issue(issue)` joins via the **nested-set** columns so a parent issue's time includes descendants (`:73–76`); `left_join_issue` joins issues but **AND-gated by `Issue.visible_condition`** (`:66–72`).

### Where spent-hours totals are computed

**Per-issue (instance, NO visibility gate):**
- `Issue#spent_hours` — `app/models/issue.rb:1178`: `time_entries.sum(:hours)` — raw sum of all entries on the issue.
- `Issue#total_spent_hours` — `app/models/issue.rb:1183`: leaf → `spent_hours`; else sums `self_and_descendants.joins(:time_entries)` over the nested set. **No `TimeEntry.visible` filter.**

**Per-issue (class, visibility-gated preloads):**
- `Issue.load_visible_spent_hours(issues, user)` — `app/models/issue.rb:1241`: `TimeEntry.visible(user).where(issue_id: ...).group(:issue_id).sum(:hours)`, then stuffs the result into each issue's `@spent_hours` ivar (`:1245`).
- `Issue.load_visible_total_spent_hours(issues, user)` — `app/models/issue.rb:1251`: same but joins parent via nested-set columns and overwrites `@total_spent_hours` (`:1258`).
- These two **override the un-gated instance methods by pre-seeding the memo ivars** (the same ivars cleared in `reload` at `issue.rb:280–281`). So whether totals respect visibility depends entirely on whether a preloader ran first.

**Per-version (NO visibility gate):**
- `Version#spent_hours` — `app/models/version.rb:255`: `TimeEntry.joins(:issue).where(fixed_version_id: id).sum(:hours)` — raw.

**Project level:** there is **no `Project#spent_hours`** (grep is empty). Project spent-time is computed through `TimeEntryQuery`/`Redmine::Helpers::TimeReport` (from Tasks 3/5), which apply `TimeEntry.visible_condition`.

### The visibility/permission gates on totals
The gate is `TimeEntry.visible` / `TimeEntry.visible_condition` — `app/models/time_entry.rb:62` / `:87` — built on `Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_time_entries)` with a role block keyed off **`role.time_entries_visibility`** (`'all'` → no extra condition; `'own'` → `user_id = me`; else `1=0`). The per-record mirror is `TimeEntry#visible?` (`:100`). This is a **third sibling** of the Task-4 visibility family (`:view_time_entries` permission + `time_entries_visibility` role attribute, parallel to issues' `:view_issues` + `issues_visibility`).

**Critical asymmetry for the planned change:** the gate is applied *inconsistently* depending on entry point:
- **Gated:** the issue list and issue show — `IssuesController` calls the preloaders at `app/controllers/issues_controller.rb:65–66` and `:108–109`; `IssueQuery#issues` calls them at `app/models/issue_query.rb:437,440`; and the spent-hours *columns* embed `TimeEntry.visible_condition` directly (Task 5, `issue_query.rb:315,329`).
- **Un-gated:** the bare `Issue#spent_hours`/`#total_spent_hours` instance methods (`:1178`,`:1183`) and `Version#spent_hours` (`:255`) sum **all** time entries regardless of `:view_time_entries`. Any caller that hits these *without* a preloader running first sees totals that may include hours from time entries the user can't view.

### Carry-forward
Time totals have a **split-brain design**: raw instance/version sums vs. visibility-gated class preloaders that override them via memo ivars. The correctness of "spent hours respects permissions" is an emergent property of controllers remembering to call `load_visible_*` first — not enforced in the aggregation methods themselves. For a visibility change, this is a prime leak site: `Issue#spent_hours`, `Issue#total_spent_hours`, and `Version#spent_hours` bypass the gate by default, and `time_entries_visibility` is a fourth role attribute (alongside `issues_visibility`) that any role-model change must carry.

Ready for Task 7.

end

  # Returns the users that should be notified
  def notified_users
    # Author and assignee are always notified unless they have been
    # locked or don't want to be notified
    notified = [author, assigned_to, previous_assignee].compact.uniq
    notified = notified.map {|n| n.is_a?(Group) ? n.users : n}.flatten
    notified.uniq!
    notified = notified.select {|u| u.active? && u.notify_about?(self)}

    notified += project.notified_users
    notified += project.users.preload(:preference).select(&:notify_about_high_priority_issues?) if priority.high?
    notified.uniq!
    # Remove users that can not view the issue
    notified.reject! {|user| !visible?(user)}
    notified
  end

  # Returns the email addresses that should be notified
  def recipients
    notified_users.collect(&:mail)
  end

  def notify?
    @notify != false
  end

  def notify=(arg)
    @notify = arg
  end

  # Returns the number of hours spent on this issue
  def spent_hours
    @spent_hours ||= time_entries.sum(:hours) || 0.0
  end

  # Returns the total number of hours spent on this issue and its descendants
  def total_spent_hours
    @total_spent_hours ||=

file.content:
def issue_add(user, issue)
    redmine_headers 'Project' => issue.project.identifier,
                    'Issue-Tracker' => issue.tracker.name,
                    'Issue-Id' => issue.id,
                    'Issue-Author' => issue.author.login,
                    'Issue-Assignee' => assignee_for_header(issue)
    redmine_headers 'Issue-Priority' => issue.priority.name if issue.priority
    message_id issue
    references issue
    @author = issue.author
    @issue = issue
    @user = user
    @issue_url = url_for(:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => issue)
    subject = "[#{issue.project.name} - #{issue.tracker.name} ##{issue.id}]"
    subject += " (#{issue.status.name})" if Setting.show_status_changes_in_mail_subject?
    subject += " #{issue.subject}"
    mail :to => user,
      :subject => subject
  end

  # Notifies users about a new issue.
  #
  # Example:
  #   Mailer.deliver_issue_add(issue)
  def self.deliver_issue_add(issue)
    users = issue.notified_users | issue.notified_watchers | issue.notified_mentions
    users.each do |user|
      issue_add(user, issue).deliver_later
    end
  end

  # Builds a mail for notifying user about an issue update
  def issue_edit(user, journal)
    issue = journal.journalized
    redmine_headers 'Project' => issue.project.identifier,
                    'Issue-Tracker' => issue.tracker.name,
                    'Issue-Id' => issue.id,
                    'Issue-Author' => issue.author.login,
                    'Issue-Assignee' => assignee_for_header(issue)
    redmine_headers 'Issue-Priority' => issue.priority.name if issue.priority
    message_id journal
    references issue
    @author = journal.user
    s = "[#{issue.project.name} - #{issue.tracker.name} ##{issue.id}] "
    s += "(#{issue.status.name}) " if journal.new_value_for('status_id') && Setting.show_status_changes_in_mail_subject?
    s += issue.subject
    @issue = issue
    @user = user
    @journal = journal
    @journal_details = journal.visible_details
    @issue_url = url_for(:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => issue, :anchor => "change-#{journal.id}")

    mail :to => user,
      :subject => s
  end

  # Notifies users about an issue update.
  #
  # Example:
  #   Mailer.deliver_issue_edit(journal)
  def self.deliver_issue_edit(journal)
    users  = journal.notified_users | journal.notified_watchers | journal.notified_mentions | journal.journalized.notified_mentions
    users.select! do |user|
      journal.notes? || journal.visible_details(user).any?
    end
    users.each do |user|
      issue_edit(user, journal).deliver_later
    end
  end

  # Builds a mail to user about a new document.
  def document_added(user, document, author)
    redmine_headers 'Project' => document.project.identifier
    @author = author
    @document = document

def send_notification
    if notify? &&
        (
          Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_updated') ||
          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_note_added') && notes.present?) ||
          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_status_updated') && new_status.present?) ||
          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_assigned_to_updated') && detail_for_attribute('assigned_to_id').present?) ||
          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_priority_updated') && new_value_for('priority_id').present?) ||
          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_fixed_version_updated') && detail_for_attribute('fixed_version_id').present?) ||
          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_attachment_added') && details.any? {|d| d.property == 'attachment' && d.value })
        )
      Mailer.deliver_issue_edit(self)
    end
  end

  def select_journal_visible_user(notified)
    if private_notes?
      notified = notified.select {|user| user.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, journalized.project)}
    end
    notified
  end
end

file.content:
def notify_about?(object)
    if mail_notification == 'all'
      true
    elsif mail_notification.blank? || mail_notification == 'none'
      false
    else
      case object
      when Issue
        case mail_notification
        when 'selected', 'only_my_events'
          # user receives notifications for created/assigned issues on unselected projects
          object.author == self || is_or_belongs_to?(object.assigned_to) || is_or_belongs_to?(object.previous_assignee)
        when 'only_assigned'
          is_or_belongs_to?(object.assigned_to) || is_or_belongs_to?(object.previous_assignee)
        when 'only_owner'
          object.author == self
        when 'only_my_watches'
          object.watched_by?(self)
        end
      when News
        # always send to project members except when mail_notification is set to 'none'
        true
      end
    end
  end

file.content:
def notified_watchers
          notified = watcher_users.active.to_a
          notified = notified.map {|n| n.is_a?(Group) ? n.users.active : n}.flatten
          notified.uniq!
          notified.reject! {|user| user.mail.blank? || user.mail_notification == 'none'}
          if respond_to?(:visible?)
            notified.reject! {|user| !visible?(user)}
          end
          notified
        end

        # Returns an array of watchers' email addresses
        def watcher_recipients
          notified_watchers.collect(&:mail)
        end

        module ClassMethods; end
      end
    end
  end

content.text:
## Task 7: Issue notification chain

Two parallel flows — **create** and **update** — both end in `ActionMailer ... deliver_later`. Order is top-to-bottom within each.

### A. Issue CREATE
1. **Trigger** — `after_create_commit :send_notification` on `Issue` — `app/models/issue.rb:132`. Note `:130–131`: it's registered *after* `create_parent_issue_journal` deliberately because `after_*` callbacks run in inverse order, so `send_notification` runs first. Fires only after the DB commit.
2. **Dispatch hop** — `Issue#send_notification` — `app/models/issue.rb:2099`: guarded by `Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_added')` (and `notify?`), then calls `Mailer.deliver_issue_add(self)` (`:2101`).
3. **Recipient assembly** — `Mailer.deliver_issue_add(issue)` — `app/models/mailer.rb:105`: union of three sets — `issue.notified_users | issue.notified_watchers | issue.notified_mentions` (`:106`) — then iterates, building+enqueuing one mail per user (`:108`, `issue_add(user, issue).deliver_later`).
4. **Compose** — `Mailer#issue_add(user, issue)` — `app/models/mailer.rb:81`: sets Redmine headers, message-id/references for threading, subject, `@issue_url`, and `mail :to => user` (`:97`).

### B. Issue UPDATE
The trigger lives on the **Journal**, not the Issue — every issue edit creates a Journal, and the Journal's commit fires the mail.
1. **Trigger** — `after_create_commit :send_notification` on `Journal` — `app/models/journal.rb:66`.
2. **Dispatch hop** — `Journal#send_notification` — `app/models/journal.rb:374`: guarded by `notify?` **and** a granular `Setting.notified_events` check per change type (`issue_updated`, `issue_note_added`, `issue_status_updated`, `issue_assigned_to_updated`, `issue_priority_updated`, `issue_fixed_version_updated`, `issue_attachment_added` — `:377–383`), then `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit(self)` (`:385`).
3. **Recipient assembly** — `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit(journal)` — `app/models/mailer.rb:141`: union `journal.notified_users | journal.notified_watchers | journal.notified_mentions | journal.journalized.notified_mentions` (`:142`), then a second filter dropping anyone who can't see *any* of the actual changes: `select! { journal.notes? || journal.visible_details(user).any? }` (`:143–145`). One `issue_edit(user, journal).deliver_later` per survivor (`:146–148`).
4. **Compose** — `Mailer#issue_edit(user, journal)` — `app/models/mailer.rb:113`: like `issue_add` but per-recipient `@journal_details = journal.visible_details` (`:130`, computed for `@user`) and a change-anchored URL (`:131`).

### Who decides the recipients (the selection logic, layered)
**Base set — `Issue#notified_users`** — `app/models/issue.rb:1148`, runs these filters in order:
1. Start with `author`, `assigned_to`, `previous_assignee` (`:1151`), expanding groups to member users (`:1152`).
2. Keep only `u.active? && u.notify_about?(self)` (`:1154`).
3. Add `project.notified_users` (`:1156`) and, if high priority, members with `notify_about_high_priority_issues?` (`:1157`).
4. **Visibility gate** — `notified.reject! { |user| !visible?(user) }` (`:1160`) — strips anyone who can't *see* the issue (Task 4's `Issue#visible?`).

**Per-user preference — `User#notify_about?(object)`** — `app/models/user.rb:864`: `'all'`→true, `'none'/blank`→false; otherwise for an `Issue` matches `mail_notification` mode against author/assignee/previous_assignee/watched (`:870–882`).

**Watchers — `notified_watchers`** — `lib/plugins/acts_as_watchable/lib/acts_as_watchable.rb:122`: active watcher_users, drops `mail_notification == 'none'`, and **applies the same `visible?` gate** (`:127–129`).

**Mentions — `notified_mentions`** — `lib/redmine/acts/mentionable.rb:45`.

### Field-level gating for updates (private notes)
For the update path, the Journal layer adds private-notes enforcement on top of every recipient set:
- `Journal#notified_users` — `app/models/journal.rb:186`: if `private_notes?`, keep only users with `:view_private_notes` (`:188–190`).
- `Journal#notified_watchers` / `#notified_mentions` (`:198`,`:203`) route through `select_journal_visible_user` (`:389`), same `:view_private_notes` filter.

### Carry-forward
Recipient selection re-applies **Task-4 issue `visible?`** at *three* independent points (`issue.rb:1160`, `acts_as_watchable.rb:128`, and the `deliver_issue_edit` change-visibility `select!` at `mailer.rb:143`), plus **field-level `:view_private_notes`** gating in the Journal layer. So the notification system is a fourth major consumer of the visibility model — and it enforces visibility *per recipient* at send time, not at query time. Any change to `visible?` semantics silently changes who gets mail. The create vs. update split (Issue callback vs. Journal callback, `issue_added` vs. seven granular `notified_events`) means the two paths must be kept in sync separately.

Ready for Task 8.

content.text: Two layers here: the `Repository` STI subclasses and the `Redmine::Scm::Adapters` layer. Let me read the base model's dispatch glue.

content.content:
20	class ScmFetchError < StandardError; end
21	
22	class Repository < ApplicationRecord
23	  include Redmine::Ciphering
24	  include Redmine::SafeAttributes
25	
26	  # Maximum length for repository identifiers
27	  IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH = 255
28	
29	  belongs_to :project
30	  has_many :changesets, lambda{order("#{Changeset.table_name}.committed_on DESC, #{Changeset.table_name}.id DESC")}
31	  has_many :filechanges, :class_name => 'Change', :through => :changesets
32	
33	  serialize :extra_info
34	
35	  before_validation :normalize_identifier
36	  before_save :check_default
37	
38	  # Raw SQL to delete changesets and changes in the database
39	  # has_many :changesets, :dependent => :destroy is too slow for big repositories
40	  before_destroy :clear_changesets
41	
42	  validates_length_of :login, maximum: 60, allow_nil: true
43	  validates_length_of :password, :maximum => 255, :allow_nil => true
44	  validates_length_of :root_url, :url, maximum: 255
45	  validates_length_of :identifier, :maximum => IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH, :allow_blank => true
46	  validates_uniqueness_of :identifier, :scope => :project_id, :case_sensitive => true
47	  validates_exclusion_of :identifier, :in => %w(browse show entry raw changes annotate diff statistics graph revisions revision)
48	  # donwcase letters, digits, dashes, underscores but not digits only
49	  validates_format_of :identifier, :with => /\A(?!\d+$)[a-z0-9\-_]*\z/, :allow_blank => true
50	  # Checks if the SCM is enabled when creating a repository
51	  validate :repo_create_validation, :on => :create
52	  validate :validate_repository_path
53	
54	  safe_attributes(
55	    'identifier',
56	    'login',
57	    'password',
58	    'path_encoding',
59	    'log_encoding',
60	    'is_default')
61	
62	  safe_attributes(
63	    'url',
64	    :if => lambda {|repository, user| repository.new_record?})
65	
66	  def repo_create_validation
67	    unless Setting.enabled_scm.include?(self.class.name.demodulize)
68	      errors.add(:type, :invalid)
69	    end
70	  end
71	
72	  def self.human_attribute_name(attribute_key_name, *)
73	    attr_name = attribute_key_name.to_s
74	    if attr_name == "log_encoding"
75	      attr_name = "commit_logs_encoding"
76	    end
77	    super(attr_name, *)
78	  end
79	
80	  # Removes leading and trailing whitespace
81	  def url=(arg)
82	    write_attribute(:url, arg ? arg.to_s.strip : nil)
83	  end

class ScmFetchError < StandardError; end

class Repository < ApplicationRecord
  include Redmine::Ciphering
  include Redmine::SafeAttributes

  # Maximum length for repository identifiers
  IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH = 255

  belongs_to :project
  has_many :changesets, lambda{order("#{Changeset.table_name}.committed_on DESC, #{Changeset.table_name}.id DESC")}
  has_many :filechanges, :class_name => 'Change', :through => :changesets

  serialize :extra_info

  before_validation :normalize_identifier
  before_save :check_default

  # Raw SQL to delete changesets and changes in the database
  # has_many :changesets, :dependent => :destroy is too slow for big repositories
  before_destroy :clear_changesets

  validates_length_of :login, maximum: 60, allow_nil: true
  validates_length_of :password, :maximum => 255, :allow_nil => true
  validates_length_of :root_url, :url, maximum: 255
  validates_length_of :identifier, :maximum => IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH, :allow_blank => true
  validates_uniqueness_of :identifier, :scope => :project_id, :case_sensitive => true
  validates_exclusion_of :identifier, :in => %w(browse show entry raw changes annotate diff statistics graph revisions revision)
  # donwcase letters, digits, dashes, underscores but not digits only
  validates_format_of :identifier, :with => /\A(?!\d+$)[a-z0-9\-_]*\z/, :allow_blank => true
  # Checks if the SCM is enabled when creating a repository
  validate :repo_create_validation, :on => :create
  validate :validate_repository_path

  safe_attributes(
    'identifier',
    'login',
    'password',
    'path_encoding',
    'log_encoding',
    'is_default')

  safe_attributes(
    'url',
    :if => lambda {|repository, user| repository.new_record?})

  def repo_create_validation
    unless Setting.enabled_scm.include?(self.class.name.demodulize)
      errors.add(:type, :invalid)
    end
  end

  def self.human_attribute_name(attribute_key_name, *)
    attr_name = attribute_key_name.to_s
    if attr_name == "log_encoding"
      attr_name = "commit_logs_encoding"
    end
    super(attr_name, *)
  end

  # Removes leading and trailing whitespace
  def url=(arg)
    write_attribute(:url, arg ? arg.to_s.strip : nil)
  end

  # Removes leading and trailing whitespace
  def root_url=(arg)
    write_attribute(:root_url, arg ? arg.to_s.strip : nil)
  end

  def password
    read_ciphered_attribute(:password)
  end

  def password=(arg)
    write_ciphered_attribute(:password, arg)
  end

  def scm_adapter
    self.class.scm_adapter_class
  end

  def scm
    unless @scm
      @scm = self.scm_adapter.new(url, root_url,
                                  login, password, path_encoding)
      if root_url.blank? && @scm.root_url.present?
        update_attribute(:root_url, @scm.root_url)
      end
    end
    @scm
  end

  def scm_name
    self.class.scm_name
  end

  def name
    if identifier.present?
      identifier
    elsif is_default?
      l(:field_repository_is_default)
    else
      scm_name
    end
  end

  def identifier=(identifier)
    super unless identifier_frozen?
  end

  def identifier_frozen?
    errors[:identifier].blank? && !(new_record? || identifier.blank?)
  end

  def identifier_param
    (identifier.presence || id.to_s)
  end

  def <=>(repository)

=== Subversion subclass ===
class Repository::Subversion < Repository
  validates_presence_of :url
  validates_format_of :url, :with => %r{\A(http|https|svn(\+[^\s:\/\\]+)?|file):\/\/.+\z}i

  def self.scm_adapter_class
    Redmine::Scm::Adapters::SubversionAdapter
  end

  def self.scm_name
    'Subversion'
  end

  def supports_directory_revisions?
    true
  end

  def repo_log_encoding
    'UTF-8'
  end

  def latest_changesets(path, rev, limit=10)
    revisions = scm.revisions(path, rev, nil, :limit => limit)
    if revisions
      identifiers = revisions.filter_map(&:identifier)
      changesets.where(:revision => identifiers).reorder("committed_on DESC").includes(:repository, :user).to_a
    else
      []
    end
  end

  # Returns a path relative to the url of the repository
  def relative_path(path)
    path.gsub(Regexp.new("^/?#{Regexp.escape(relative_url)}"), '')
  end

  def fetch_changesets
    scm_info = scm.info
    if scm_info
      # latest revision found in database
      db_revision = latest_changeset ? latest_changeset.revision.to_i : 0
      # latest revision in the repository
      scm_revision = scm_info.lastrev.identifier.to_i
      if db_revision < scm_revision
        logger.debug "Fetching changesets for repository #{url}" if logger && logger.debug?
        identifier_from = db_revision + 1
        while (identifier_from <= scm_revision)
          # loads changesets by batches of 200
          identifier_to = [identifier_from + 199, scm_revision].min
          revisions = scm.revisions('', identifier_to, identifier_from, :with_paths => true)

# Can be called periodically by an external script
  # eg. ruby script/runner "Repository.fetch_changesets"
  def self.fetch_changesets
    Project.active.has_module(:repository).all.each do |project|
      project.repositories.each do |repository|
        begin
          repository.fetch_changesets
        rescue Redmine::Scm::Adapters::CommandFailed => e
          logger.error "scm: error during fetching changesets: #{e.message}"
        end
      end
    end
  end

  # scan changeset comments to find related and fixed issues for all repositories
  def self.scan_changesets_for_issue_ids
    all.each(&:scan_changesets_for_issue_ids)
  end

  def self.scm_name
    'Abstract'
  end

  def self.available_scm
    subclasses.collect {|klass| [klass.scm_name, klass.name]}
  end

  def self.factory(klass_name, *)
    repository_class(klass_name).new(*) rescue nil
  end

  def self.repository_class(class_name)
    class_name = class_name.to_s.camelize
    if Redmine::Scm::Base.all.include?(class_name)
      "Repository::#{class_name}".constantize
    end
  end

  def self.scm_adapter_class
    nil
  end

  def self.scm_command
    ret = ""
    begin
      ret = self.scm_adapter_class.client_command if self.scm_adapter_class
    rescue => e
      logger.error "scm: error during get command: #{e.message}"
    end
    ret
  end

  def self.scm_version_string
    ret = ""
    begin

content.text:
## Task 8: Repository → SCM backend dispatch

Redmine uses a **two-tier polymorphism** for VCS: ActiveRecord STI for the *record*, and a separate adapter class hierarchy for the *shell-out client*. The record dispatches to the right backend via STI subclass + a `scm_adapter_class` indirection.

### Tier 1 — the record (STI)
`Repository < ApplicationRecord` — `app/models/repository.rb:22` is the abstract base. Concrete subclasses live in **`app/models/repository/`**, one file per system:
- `Repository::Subversion` — `app/models/repository/subversion.rb:22`
- `Repository::Git` — `app/models/repository/git.rb:23`
- `Repository::Mercurial` — `app/models/repository/mercurial.rb`
- `Repository::Cvs` — `app/models/repository/cvs.rb`
- `Repository::Bazaar` — `app/models/repository/bazaar.rb`
- `Repository::Filesystem` — `app/models/repository/filesystem.rb`

The `type` column drives Rails STI, so loading a repo row instantiates the correct subclass automatically.

### Tier 2 — the SCM adapter (the actual backend)
Each subclass declares its backend by **overriding `self.scm_adapter_class`** — base returns `nil` (`repository.rb:407`), e.g. `Repository::Subversion.scm_adapter_class` returns `Redmine::Scm::Adapters::SubversionAdapter` (`app/models/repository/subversion.rb:26–28`). The adapters live **separately, under `lib/redmine/scm/adapters/`** (e.g. `lib/redmine/scm/adapters/git_adapter.rb:45`, plus `subversion_adapter.rb`, `mercurial_adapter.rb`, …), all descending from a shared `Redmine::Scm::Adapters::AbstractAdapter`.

### How an operation is dispatched (the hops)
1. **Instance accessor `Repository#scm`** — `app/models/repository.rb:102–111`: lazily builds `@scm = self.scm_adapter.new(url, root_url, login, password, path_encoding)`.
2. **`Repository#scm_adapter`** — `repository.rb:98–100`: just delegates to `self.class.scm_adapter_class` — i.e. the STI subclass picks the adapter class. This is the pivot from record-type to backend-type.
3. **The operation itself** is a normal Ruby override. `fetch_changesets` is defined per subclass (`Repository::Subversion#fetch_changesets` at `subversion.rb:57`, `Repository::Filesystem` at `filesystem.rb:48`, `Bazaar` at `bazaar.rb:89`, `Cvs` at `cvs.rb:128`, `Mercurial` at `mercurial.rb:199`) and **inside it calls `scm.<command>`** — e.g. SVN's calls `scm.info` and `scm.revisions(...)` (`subversion.rb:58,69`), shelling out via the adapter. The base `Repository` has **no instance `fetch_changesets`** — it's abstract; each subclass must implement it.

### Where the operation is triggered
- **Class-level cron entry** `Repository.fetch_changesets` — `app/models/repository.rb:371`: iterates active projects with the `:repository` module, and for each repo calls the **instance** `repository.fetch_changesets` (`:375`) — which dynamically dispatches to the STI subclass override. Rescues `Redmine::Scm::Adapters::CommandFailed` (`:376`).
- **Web/sys trigger** `SysController#fetch_changesets` — `app/controllers/sys_controller.rb:54` (the reposman/external hook).

### Supporting factory & registry glue
- `Repository.factory(klass_name)` — `repository.rb:396` → `repository_class` (`:400`): camelizes the name, validates against `Redmine::Scm::Base.all` (the enabled-SCM registry), and `constantize`s to `Repository::#{class_name}` — how a chosen SCM string becomes the right subclass at creation.
- `Repository.available_scm` — `:392`: maps `subclasses` to `[scm_name, class_name]` for the UI.
- `scm_command` / `scm_version_string` / `scm_available` (`:411`,`:421`,`:431`) — all delegate through `scm_adapter_class` to the adapter's `client_*` class methods, again routing record→adapter.
- `repo_create_validation` (`:66`) gates creation on `Setting.enabled_scm.include?(self.class.name.demodulize)`.

### Carry-forward (relevance to the visibility change)
This subsystem is a clean **STI dispatch over `scm_adapter_class`**, structurally independent of the permission model — its access control is coarse-grained: the `:repository` *project module* (checked in `Repository.fetch_changesets` via `has_module(:repository)`) and `RepositoriesController` permissions, not per-record `visible?`. The relevant link back to the visibility work is `Changeset#fix_issue`/`find_referenced_issue_by_id` (Task 3) — commits reference issues, so changeset→issue rendering must still honor issue visibility even though repositories themselves don't use the `visible_condition` machinery. The per-system definitions are split across two trees that change in lockstep: `app/models/repository/*.rb` (record) and `lib/redmine/scm/adapters/*.rb` (backend).

Ready for Task 9.

## Task 9: Impact map — changing issue visibility & permissions

Synthesis of all eight walks. Ranked by risk = (likelihood a change breaks it) × (severity = silent data leak > broken feature > cosmetic). **Tier 1 = touch-or-leak; verify first.**

---

### TIER 0 — The rule definition itself (the change epicenter)
These *are* the thing being changed. Every edit starts here and must keep the SQL and Ruby forms logically identical.

| What | file:line | Why critical |
|---|---|---|
| `Issue.visible_condition` (SQL builder) | `app/models/issue.rb:137` | DB-level rule: `issues_visibility` × privacy/author/assignee × per-tracker. Feeds `scope :visible` (`issue.rb:84`). |
| `Issue#visible?` (per-record) | `app/models/issue.rb:172` | Ruby mirror — **must match `:137` case-for-case** or list/show disagree. |
| `Project.allowed_to_condition` (shared engine) | `app/models/project.rb:189` | The primitive both forms delegate to (builtin role, `project_ids_by_role`, module-enabled, admin bypass). Any signature/semantics change ripples to *all* `visible_condition`s. |
| `Project#visible?` / `Project.visible_condition` | `app/models/project.rb:168` / `:178` | Issues are gated by project visibility first. |

---

### TIER 1 — Silent-leak surfaces (highest severity: wrong = data disclosure)
Each independently re-applies or bypasses the rule. A miss here ships a leak, not a crash.

1. **`IssueQuery#base_scope`** — `app/models/issue_query.rb:375` (`Issue.visible.joins(...)`). The canonical list gate; every index/Gantt/report list flows through it.
2. **Notification recipients re-apply `visible?` per-user at send time** — three points that must all track the rule:
   - `Issue#notified_users` reject — `app/models/issue.rb:1160`
   - watcher gate — `lib/plugins/acts_as_watchable/lib/acts_as_watchable.rb:128`
   - update change-visibility `select!` — `app/models/mailer.rb:143`
   - plus field-level private notes: `Journal#notified_users` `app/models/journal.rb:189`, `select_journal_visible_user` `:389`.
3. **Time-total leak via un-gated aggregators** — `Issue#spent_hours` `app/models/issue.rb:1178`, `#total_spent_hours` `:1183`, `Version#spent_hours` `app/models/version.rb:255` sum **all** entries; safety depends on preloaders `load_visible_spent_hours`/`load_visible_total_spent_hours` (`issue.rb:1241`,`:1251`) running first (callers: `issues_controller.rb:65,108`; `issue_query.rb:437,440`). Verify no path renders totals without them.
4. **Column subqueries embed visibility conditions directly**:
   - `total_estimated_hours` uses `Issue.visible_condition` (string-rewritten) — `app/models/issue_query.rb:62`
   - spent/total-spent columns use `TimeEntry.visible_condition` — `:315`,`:329`
   - permission-gated columns — `:view_time_entries` `:308`, `:set_issues_private` `:341`
5. **Single-record controller gates** — `ApplicationController#find_issue` / `#find_issues` `app/controllers/application_controller.rb:394`/`:407` (every issue action funnels here).
6. **Cross-issue leak vectors** — `IssueRelationsController#find_issue` `app/controllers/issue_relations_controller.rb:102`; `Issue.load_visible_relations` `app/models/issue.rb:1264`; `AutoCompletesController#issues` `app/controllers/auto_completes_controller.rb:23`; `SearchController#index` `app/controllers/search_controller.rb:24` + `acts_as_searchable` `lib/plugins/acts_as_searchable/lib/acts_as_searchable.rb:84`.
7. **Activity feeds query issues independently** — `acts_as_activity_provider` (`issue.rb:59`) and `acts_as_event` (`:55`); `ActivitiesControllerTest` private-notes test is the canary (`test/functional/activities_controller_test.rb:246`).
8. **Webhooks / mail handler outbound** — `Issue::Webhookable` `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20`; `MailHandler#receive_issue*` `app/models/mail_handler.rb:190`,`:232`; `Mailer.reminders` `app/models/mailer.rb:603`.

---

### TIER 2 — The sibling visibility systems that must move in lockstep
The rule isn't issue-only; it's a *family* keyed off role attributes. A schema/role change to `issues_visibility` likely needs parallel handling of these.

- **TimeEntry** (`time_entries_visibility`): `TimeEntry.visible_condition` `app/models/time_entry.rb:87`, `#visible?` `:100`, `scope :left_join_issue` (embeds `Issue.visible_condition`) `:66`.
- **Custom-field-level visibility**: `CustomField#visibility_by_project_condition` `app/models/custom_field.rb:278`, `IssueCustomField` `app/models/issue_custom_field.rb:35`, `ProjectCustomField` `app/models/project_custom_field.rb:29`; reached by column builder via `issue_custom_fields.visible` (`query.rb:692` + `custom_field.rb:56`).
- **Private notes** (`:view_private_notes`): `Journal#visible?` `app/models/journal.rb:165`, `#visible_details` `:122`.
- **Ruby-only `visible?` peers** sharing `User#allowed_to?`: `News` `app/models/news.rb:53`, `Document` `:55`, `Message` `:78`, `WikiPage` `:89`.
- **Query SQL role filters**: `IssueQuery#sql_for_assigned_to_role_field` `app/models/issue_query.rb:599`, `sql_for_author_role_field` `:637`, `sql_for_is_private_field` `:671`, `sql_for_watcher_id_field` `:555`; `TimeEntryQuery#sql_for_user_role_field` `app/models/time_entry_query.rb:309`.

---

### TIER 3 — Role/permission plumbing feeding the rule (verify inputs)
The rule reads these; if their shape changes, Tier 0 breaks. (The Role/Member/`Redmine::AccessControl` registry — Task 1's seam at `lib/redmine/access_control.rb` — is where new permissions/role attributes are declared.)
- `role.issues_visibility`, `role.time_entries_visibility` (Role model).
- `role.permissions_all_trackers?` / `permissions_tracker_ids?` — consumed at `issue.rb:159–166`,`:189–190`.
- `User#allowed_to?`, `User#project_ids_by_role`, `User#builtin_role`, `is_or_belongs_to?` — `app/models/user.rb` (esp. group expansion; `assigned_to` is a `Principal`, so group membership matters everywhere assignee appears).
- `User#notify_about?` `app/models/user.rb:864` (mail-preference filter layered on visibility).

---

### TIER 4 — Mass mutation & field-write gates (correctness, not disclosure)
- `Issue` mass-assignment: `Redmine::SafeAttributes` `lib/redmine/safe_attributes.rb:21`; `safe_attributes` decls `issue.rb:498–565`, `safe_attributes=` `:565`. Field-level write visibility lives here.
- Edit/delete permission helpers: `attributes_editable?` `issue.rb:207`, `notes_addable?` `:223`, `deletable?` `:228`, attachment gates `:218`,`:234`.
- Bulk ops: `IssuesController#bulk_edit`/`bulk_update` `app/controllers/issues_controller.rb:262`/`:349`.

---

### TIER 5 — Low risk / out of scope (note, don't re-verify deeply)
- **Version control**: STI dispatch over `scm_adapter_class` (`app/models/repository.rb:407`, subclasses `app/models/repository/*.rb`, adapters `lib/redmine/scm/adapters/*.rb`) is **independent of per-record visibility** — gated only by the `:repository` module. *Only* touchpoint: changeset→issue references `Changeset#fix_issue` `app/models/changeset.rb:238`, `#find_referenced_issue_by_id` `:212` must still honor issue visibility when rendered (`ApplicationHelper#parse_redmine_links` `app/helpers/application_helper.rb:1145`, `#format_object` `:268`).
- Migrations/importers (`migrate_from_*.rake`, `IssueImport` `app/models/issue_import.rb:113`) — data-shape only.

---

### Test oracles to gate the change (run these)
- `test/functional/issues_controller_test.rb` (esp. `test_post_create_private:4941`) — list/show/private.
- `test/functional/attachments_visibility_test.rb:24` — attachment leakage.
- `test/functional/issue_relations_controller_test.rb:166` ("visible issues only").
- `test/functional/activities_controller_test.rb:246` — private notes in activity.
- Add: a test asserting `Issue.visible_condition` and `Issue#visible?` agree on the same fixtures (the duplication has no enforcement today).

---

### One-paragraph scope for the teammate
The change centers on **one rule expressed twice** (`issue.rb:137` SQL + `:172` Ruby) over **one shared engine** (`project.rb:189`). It leaks if you miss any of ~12 independent re-application sites — list scope (`issue_query.rb:375`), four notification gates (`issue.rb:1160`, `acts_as_watchable.rb:128`, `mailer.rb:143`, `journal.rb:189`), three un-gated time aggregators (`issue.rb:1178/1183`, `version.rb:255`), the column subqueries (`issue_query.rb:62/315/329`), relations/autocomplete/search/activity/webhook paths, and the single-record controller filters (`application_controller.rb:394/407`). It must move in lockstep with the **sibling visibility systems** (TimeEntry, custom fields, private notes) because they share the role-attribute pattern and the `allowed_to_condition` primitive. Version control is out of scope except changeset→issue link rendering. Start at Tier 0, prove SQL↔Ruby parity with a new fixture test, then walk Tier 1 leak sites one by one.

This completes the 9-task deep-dive.
